records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. From the previous chain from al-Hassan from Ali b. Shajarah from some of the companions Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “Whenever you recite Surah Lahab, curse Abu Lahab without fail because he was such a liar that he had denied the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) and whatever was revealed upon him.”
records.arabicText: بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ عَنْ عَلِیِّ بْنِ شَجَرَهَ عَنْ بَعْضِ أَصْحَابِ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: إِذَا قَرَأْتُمْ تَبَّتْ یَدا أَبِی لَهَبٍ وَ تَبَ فَادْعُوا عَلَی أَبِی لَهَبٍ فَإِنَّهُ کَانَ مِنَ الْمُکَذِّبِینَ الَّذِینَ یُکَذِّبُونَ بِالنَّبِیِّ ص وَ بِمَا جَاءَ بِهِ مِنْ عِنْدِ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. From the previous chain from al-Hassan from Ali b. Shajarah from some of the companions Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “Whenever you recite Surah Lahab, curse Abu Lahab without fail because he was such a liar that he had denied the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) and whatever was revealed upon him.”

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. From the previous chain from Sayf b. ’Umayra from Mansur b. Hazim from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “One who offers five times prayer daily but does not recite Surah Tauheed would be told, ‘O servant of Allah! You are not a reciter of Salaat’?”
records.arabicText: بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ عَنْ سَیْفِ بْنِ عَمِیرَهَ عَنْ مَنْصُورِ بْنِ حَازِمٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ مَضَی بِهِ یَوْمٌ وَاحِدٌ فَصَلَّی فِیهِ خَمْسَ صَلَوَاتٍ وَ لَمْ یَقْرَأْ فِیهَا بِ قُلْ هُوَ اللَّهُ أَحَدٌ قِیلَ لَهُ یَا عَبْدَ اللَّهِ لَسْتَ مِنَ الْمُصَلِّینَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. From the previous chain from Sayf b. ’Umayra from Mansur b. Hazim from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One who offers five times prayer daily but does not recite Surah Tauheed would be told, ‘O servant of Allah! You are not a reciter of Salaat’?”

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 2. And from the previous chain from al-Hassan from Abi Abdillah from Ishaq b. Ammar from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “One who dies on Friday without having recited Surah Tauheed has died having faith on the religion of Abu Lahab.”
records.arabicText: وَ بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ إِسْحَاقَ بْنِ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ مَضَتْ لَهُ جُمْعَهٌ وَ لَمْ یَقْرَأْ فِیهَا بِ قُلْ هُوَ اللَّهُ أَحَدٌ ثُمَّ مَاتَ مَاتَ عَلَی دِینِ أَبِی لَهَبٍ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 2. And from the previous chain from al-Hassan from Abi Abdillah from Ishaq b. Ammar from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One who dies on Friday without having recited Surah Tauheed has died having faith on the religion of Abu Lahab.”

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 3. And from the previous chain from al-Hassan from Sandal from Harun b. Kharija from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “One who is involved in some sort of disease or problem and dies without reciting Surah Tauheed is condemned to Hell.”
records.arabicText: وَ بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ عَنْ صَنْدَلٍ عَنْ هَارُونَ بْنِ خَارِجَهَ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ أَصَابَهُ مَرَضٌ أَوْ شِدَّهٌ وَ لَمْ یَقْرَأْ فِی مَرَضِهِ أَوْ شِدَّتِهِ بِ قُلْ هُوَ اللَّهُ أَحَدٌ ثُمَّ مَاتَ فِی مَرَضِهِ أَوْ فِی تِلْکَ الشِّدَّهِ الَّتِی نَزَلَتْ بِهِ فَهُوَ مِنْ أَهْلِ النَّارِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 3. And from the previous chain from al-Hassan from Sandal from Harun b. Kharija from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One who is involved in some sort of disease or problem and dies without reciting Surah Tauheed is condemned to Hell.”

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 4. And from the previous chain from al-Hassan from Sayf b. ’Umayra from Ibn [Abi] Bakr al-Hadhrami from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “One who has faith on Allah and the Day of Judgment it is necessary for him to recite Surah Tauheed in his obligatory prayers. If a person does so Allah will grant him goodness in this world and the hereafter and will forgive him, his parents and his children.”
records.arabicText: وَ بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ عَنْ سَیْفِ بْنِ عَمِیرَهَ عَنْ ابن [أَبِی] بَکْرٍ الْحَضْرَمِیِّ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ کانَ یُؤْمِنُ بِاللَّهِ وَ الْیَوْمِ الْآخِرِ فَلَا یَدَعْ أَنْ یَقْرَأَ فِی دُبُرِ الْفَرِیضَهِ بِ قُلْ هُوَ اللَّهُ أَحَدٌ فَإِنَّهُ مَنْ قَرَأَهَا جَمَعَ اللَّهُ لَهُ خَیْرَ الدُّنْیَا وَ الْآخِرَهِ وَ غَفَرَ اللَّهُ لَهُ وَ لِوَالِدَیْهِ وَ مَا وَلَدَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 4. And from the previous chain from al-Hassan from Sayf b. ’Umayra from Ibn [Abi] Bakr al-Hadhrami from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One who has faith on Allah and the Day of Judgment it is necessary for him to recite Surah Tauheed in his obligatory prayers. If a person does so Allah will grant him goodness in this world and the hereafter and will forgive him, his parents and his children.”

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 5.  It is narrated from Amirul Momineen (a.s.) that the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) said, “One who recites Surah Tauheed a hundred times before going to bed, Allah will forgive sins committed by him in the past fifty years.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ یَحْیَی قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ هِلَالٍ عَنْ عِیسَی بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ جَدِّهِ عَنْ أَمِیرِ الْمُؤْمِنِینَ ع قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص مَنْ قَرَأَ قُلْ هُوَ اللَّهُ أَحَدٌ مِائَهَ مَرَّهٍ حِینَ یَأْخُذُ مَضْجَعَهُ غَفَرَ اللَّهُ لَهُ ذُنُوبَ خَمْسِینَ سَنَهً.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 5.  It is narrated from Amirul Momineen (a.s.) that the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) said,
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One who recites Surah Tauheed a hundred times before going to bed, Allah will forgive sins committed by him in the past fifty years.”

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 6. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan who said it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan al-Saffar from Ibrahim b. Hashim from al-Nawfali from al-Sakuni from Ja’far b. Muhammad from his father (a.s.) who said: “His funeral prayer was attended by ninety thousand angels accompanied by Jibraeel (a.s.). I asked Jibraeel (a.s.), ‘Why did you people attend this funeral prayer?’ Jibraeel (a.s.) replied, ‘He used to recite Surah Tauheed while standing or sitting, riding or walking and while coming and going’.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ الصَّفَّارُ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِیمَ بْنِ هَاشِمٍ عَنِ النَّوْفَلِیِّ عَنِ السَّکُونِیِّ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِیهِ ع قَالَ: إِنَّ النَّبِیَّ ص صَلَّی عَلَی سَعْدِ بْنِ مُعَاذٍ فَقَالَ لَقَدْ وَافَی مِنَ الْمَلَائِکَهِ تِسْعُونَ أَلْفَ مَلَکٍ وَ فِیهِمْ جَبْرَئِیلُ ع یُصَلُّونَ عَلَیْهِ فَقُلْتُ لَهُ یَا جَبْرَئِیلُ بِمَا اسْتَحَقَّ صَلَاتَکَ عَلَیْهِ فَقَالَ بِقِرَاءَهِ قُلْ هُوَ اللَّهُ أَحَدٌ قَائِماً وَ قَاعِداً وَ رَاکِباً وَ مَاشِیاً وَ ذَاهِباً وَ جَائِیاً.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 6. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan who said it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan al-Saffar from Ibrahim b. Hashim from al-Nawfali from al-Sakuni from Ja’far b. Muhammad from his father (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “His funeral prayer was attended by ninety thousand angels accompanied by Jibraeel (a.s.). I asked Jibraeel (a.s.), ‘Why did you people attend this funeral prayer?’ Jibraeel (a.s.) replied, ‘He used to recite Surah Tauheed while standing or sitting, riding or walking and while coming and going’.”

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 7. And from the previous chain from Abi al-Hassan from Aban b. Uthman from Qays b. al-Rabee’ from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “One who recites Surah Tauheed eleven times before going to bed Allah will protect his house along with all the neighboring houses.”
records.arabicText: وَ بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ عَنْ أَبِی الْحَسَنِ عَنْ أَبَانِ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ عَنْ قَیْسِ بْنِ الرَّبِیعِ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ آوَی إِلَی فِرَاشِهِ فَقَرَأَ قُلْ هُوَ اللَّهُ أَحَدٌ إِحْدَی عَشْرَهَ مَرَّهً حَفِظَهُ اللَّهُ فِی دَارِهِ وَ فِی دُوَیْرَاتٍ حَوْلَهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 7. And from the previous chain from Abi al-Hassan from Aban b. Uthman from Qays b. al-Rabee’ from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One who recites Surah Tauheed eleven times before going to bed Allah will protect his house along with all the neighboring houses.”

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 8.  Amirul Momineen (a.s.) said, “One who recites Surah Qul huwallaahu ah’ad eleven times after Morning Prayer won’t commit any sin during that day even if the Satan tries hard. In this way, the Satan will be disgraced.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِیهِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ أَبِی الْحَسَنِ النَّهْدِیِّ عَنْ رَجُلٍ عَنْ فُضَیْلِ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ قَالَ أَخْبَرَنِی رَجُلٌ عَنْ عَمَّارِ بْنِ جَهْمٍ الزَّیَّاتِ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ حَیٍّ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَمِیرَ الْمُؤْمِنِینَ ع یَقُولُ مَنْ قَرَأَ قُلْ هُوَ اللَّهُ أَحَدٌ إِحْدَی عَشْرَهَ مَرَّهً فِی دُبُرِ الْفَجْرِ لَمْ یَتْبَعْهُ فِی ذَلِکَ الْیَوْمِ ذَنْبٌ وَ إِنْ رَغِمَ أَنْفُ الشَّیْطَانِ.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 9.  Imam  Musa  Kadhim  (a.s.)  said,  “One  who  recites  Surah Tauheed before meeting an unjust person, Allah will protect him from all sides and he will remain safe from the mischief of  that  unjust  person.  He  will  benefit  from  meeting  that unjust  person.  Whenever  you  develop  fear  of  something, recite  a  hundred  verses  of  the  Holy  Quran  and  say,  ‘O Allah! Take this calamity away from me’ thrice.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره عَنْ سَعْدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِیٍّ عَنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ جَهْمٍ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِیمَ بْنِ مرزم [مِهْزَمٍ] عَنْ رَجُلٍ سَمِعَ أَبَا الْحَسَنِ ع یَقُولُ مَنْ قَدَّمَ قُلْ هُوَ اللَّهُ بَیْنَهُ وَ بَیْنَ جَبَّارٍ مَنَعَهُ اللَّهُ مِنْهُ بِقِرَاءَتِهِ بَیْنَ یَدَیْهِ وَ مِنْ خَلْفِهِ وَ عَنْ یَمِینِهِ وَ عَنْ شِمَالِهِ فَإِذَا فَعَلَ ذَلِکَ رَزَقَهُ اللَّهُ خَیْرَهُ وَ مَنَعَهُ شَرَّهُ وَ قَالَ إِذَا خِفْتَ أَحَداً فَاقْرَأْ مِائَهَ آیَهٍ مِنَ الْقُرْآنِ مِنْ حَیْثُ شِئْتَ ثُمَّ قال [قُلْ] اللَّهُمَّ اکْشِفْ عَنِّی الْبَلَاءَ ثَلَاثَ مَرَّاتٍ.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 10. Abi (rah) from Muhammad b. Yahya al-’Attar from Muhammad b. Ahmad from al-Hassan b. Ali b. Abi Uthman from a man from Hafs b. Ghiyath who said: “O Hafs! One from our Shias who dies without having a good knowledge of recitation of the Holy Quran, is taught the Holy Quran in grave so that Allah grants him great ranks. Know that the levels of Paradise are equal to the number of verses in the Holy Quran. A reciter of the Holy Quran is told over there, ‘Recite a verse of the Holy Quran and go to the next level’.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ یَحْیَی الْعَطَّارِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِیِّ بْنِ أَبِی عُثْمَانَ عَنْ رَجُلٍ عَنْ حَفْصِ بْنِ غِیَاثٍ قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع یَقُولُ لِلرَّجُلِ أَ تُحِبُّ الْبَقَاءَ فِی الدُّنْیَا قَالَ نَعَمْ قَالَ وَ لِمَ قَالَ لِقِرَاءَهِ قُلْ هُوَ اللَّهُ فَسَکَتَ عَنْهُ ثُمَّ قَالَ مِنْ بَعْدِ سَاعَهٍ یَا حَفْصُ مَنْ مَاتَ مِنْ أَوْلِیَائِنَا وَ شِیعَتِنَا وَ لَمْ یُحْسِنِ الْقُرْآنَ عُلِّمَ فِی قَبْرِهِ لِیَرْفَعَ اللَّهُ بِهِ دَرَجَتَهُ فَإِنَّ دَرَجَاتِ الْجَنَّهِ عَلَی قَدْرِ عَدَدِ آیَاتِ الْقُرْآنِ فَیُقَالُ لِقَارِئِ الْقُرْآنِ اقْرَأْ وَ ارْقَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 10. Abi (rah) from Muhammad b. Yahya al-’Attar from Muhammad b. Ahmad from al-Hassan b. Ali b. Abi Uthman from a man from Hafs b. Ghiyath who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “O Hafs! One from our Shias who dies without having a good knowledge of recitation of the Holy Quran, is taught the Holy Quran in grave so that Allah grants him great ranks. Know that the levels of Paradise are equal to the number of verses in the Holy Quran. A reciter of the Holy Quran is told over there, ‘Recite a verse of the Holy Quran and go to the next level’.”

records.chapter: Reward of reciting Surah Naas and Surah Falaq
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Ahmad b. Idris from Muhammad b. Ahmad from Muhammad b. Ahmad from Ismail b. Mihran from al-Hassan from al-Hussain b. Abi al-’Ala from Abi ’Ubayda al-Hadha from Abi Ja’far (a.s.) who said: One who recites in Watr Prayer Surah Falaq and Surah Naas with Surah Tauheed, it is said to him: O Servant of Allah! Congratulations! Allah has accepted your Midnight Prayer.
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی أَحْمَدُ بْنُ إِدْرِیسَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ حَسَّانَ عَنْ إِسْمَاعِیلَ بْنِ مِهْرَانَ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ عَنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ أَبِی الْعَلَاءِ عَنْ أَبِی عُبَیْدَهَ الْحَذَّاءِ عَنْ أَبِی جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ: مَنْ أَوْتَرَ بِالْمُعَوِّذَتَیْنِ وَ قُلْ هُوَ اللَّهُ أَحَدٌ قِیلَ لَهُ یَا عَبْدَ اللَّهِ أَبْشِرْ فَقَدْ قَبِلَ اللَّهُ وَتْرَکَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Ahmad b. Idris from Muhammad b. Ahmad from Muhammad b. Ahmad from Ismail b. Mihran from al-Hassan from al-Hussain b. Abi al-’Ala from Abi ’Ubayda al-Hadha from Abi Ja’far (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: One who recites in Watr Prayer Surah Falaq and Surah Naas with Surah Tauheed, it is said to him: O Servant of Allah! Congratulations! Allah has accepted your Midnight Prayer.

records.chapter: Reward of abstinence from greater sins
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Musa b. Ja’far b. Wahab al-Baghdadi from al-Hassan b. Ali al-Washa from Ahmad b. Umar al-Halabi who said: The narrator asked Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) the meaning of the verse ‘If you shun the great sins which you are forbidden, We will do away with your small’. Imam (a.s.) replied, “One who keeps away from such sins due to which Allah throws one in Hell, Allah will forgive his sins and make him enter a good place (Paradise). There are seven greater sins, which condemn a person to Hell – killing an innocent, disobeying parents, taking interest, apostasy, accusing a virtuous woman of adultery, usurping the property of orphans and running away from Jihad.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ مُوسَی بْنِ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ وَهْبٍ الْبَغْدَادِیِّ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِیٍّ الْوَشَّاءِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ عُمَرَ الْحَلَبِیِّ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع عَنْ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ- إِنْ تَجْتَنِبُوا کَبائِرَ ما تُنْهَوْنَ عَنْهُ نُکَفِّرْ عَنْکُمْ سَیِّئاتِکُمْ قَالَ مَنِ اجْتَنَبَ مَا أَوْعَدَ اللَّهُ عَلَیْهِ النَّارَ إِذَا کَانَ مُؤْمِناً کَفَّرَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ سَیِّئَاتِهِ وَ یُدْخِلُهُ مُدْخَلًا کَرِیماً وَ الْکَبَائِرُ السَّبْعُ الْمُوجِبَاتُ قَتْلُ النَّفْسِ الْحَرَامِ وَ عُقُوقُ الْوَالِدَیْنِ وَ أَکْلُ الرِّبَا وَ التَّعَرُّبُ بَعْدَ الْهِجْرَهِ وَ قَذْفُ الْمُحْصَنَهِ وَ أَکْلُ مَالِ الْیَتِیمِ وَ الْفِرَارُ مِنَ الزَّحْفِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Musa b. Ja’far b. Wahab al-Baghdadi from al-Hassan b. Ali al-Washa from Ahmad b. Umar al-Halabi who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: The narrator asked Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) the meaning of the verse ‘If you shun the great sins which you are forbidden, We will do away with your small’. Imam (a.s.) replied, “One who keeps away from such sins due to which Allah throws one in Hell, Allah will forgive his sins and make him enter a good place (Paradise). There are seven greater sins, which condemn a person to Hell – killing an innocent, disobeying parents, taking interest, apostasy, accusing a virtuous woman of adultery, usurping the property of orphans and running away from Jihad.”

records.chapter: Reward of abstinence from greater sins
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 2.  Imam Ridha (a.s.) says regarding the verse ‘If you shun the great sins which you are forbidden, We will do away with your small, “One who abstains from those sins for which Allah has promised Hell, Allah will forgive his (lesser) sins.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره عَنْ سَعْدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عِیسَی عَنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ سَعِیدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْفُضَیْلِ عَنْ أَبِی الْحَسَنِ الرِّضَا ع فِی قَوْلِ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ- إِنْ تَجْتَنِبُوا کَبائِرَ ما تُنْهَوْنَ عَنْهُ نُکَفِّرْ عَنْکُمْ سَیِّئاتِکُمْ قَالَ مَنِ اجْتَنَبَ مَا أَوْعَدَ اللَّهُ عَلَیْهِ النَّارَ إِذَا کَانَ مُؤْمِناً کَفَّرَ عَنْهُ سَیِّئَاتِهِ.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  It  is  narrated  that  Imam  Ja’far  Sadiq  (a.s.)  said,  “Allah revealed onto Prophet Dawood, ‘O Dawood! If a believer commits sins and repents for it and feels ashamed in front of Me  while  repenting  I  forgive  him.  I  will  make  the  angel forget this sin and convert this sin into a good deed. I do not care (it is not difficult for me) for I am the Most Merciful’.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُوسَی بْنِ الْمُتَوَکِّلِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُوسَی بْنُ عِمْرَانَ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا الْحُسَیْنُ بْنُ یَزِیدَ عَنْ عَلِیِّ بْنِ أَبِی حَمْزَهَ عَنْ أَبِی بَصِیرٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ سَمِعْتُهُ یَقُولُ أَوْحَی اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ إِلَی دَاوُدَ النَّبِیِّ ع یَا دَاوُدُ إِنَّ عَبْدِیَ الْمُؤْمِنَ إِذَا أَذْنَبَ ذَنْباً ثُمَّ رَجَعَ وَ تَابَ مِنْ ذَلِکَ الذَّنْبِ وَ اسْتَحْیَا مِنِّی عِنْدَ ذِکْرِهِ غَفَرْتُ لَهُ وَ أَنْسَیْتُهُ الْحَفَظَهَ وَ أَبْدَلْتُهُ الْحَسَنَهَ وَ لَا أُبَالِی وَ أَنَا أَرْحَمُ الرَّاحِمِینَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1.  It  is  narrated  that  Imam  Ja’far  Sadiq  (a.s.)  said,
records.thaqalaynMatn: “Allah revealed onto Prophet Dawood, ‘O Dawood! If a believer commits sins and repents for it and feels ashamed in front of Me  while  repenting  I  forgive  him.  I  will  make  the  angel forget this sin and convert this sin into a good deed. I do not care (it is not difficult for me) for I am the Most Merciful’.”

records.chapter: Reward of condoning a loanee for the sake of Allah’s Respect
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam Musa Kadhim (a.s.) narrates that the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) said, “One who takes a loanee to a ruler and he knows that he has taken a false oath; but due to the sake of the respect of Allah, the Mighty and Sublime, he condones the loanee, Allah would not be satisfied with nothing less than giving  him  the  position of  Ibrahim, the  friend of the Beneficent.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا إِبْرَاهِیمُ بْنُ هَاشِمٍ عَنْ عَلِیِّ بْنِ سَعِیدٍ عَنْ دُرُسْتَ عَنْ عَبْدِ الْحَمِیدِ الطَّائِیِّ عَنْ أَبِی الْحَسَنِ الْأَوَّلِ ع قَالَ قَالَ النَّبِیُّ ص مَنْ قَدَّمَ غَرِیماً إِلَی السُّلْطَانِ یَسْتَحْلِفُهُ وَ هُوَ یَعْلَمُ أَنَّهُ یَحْلِفُ ثُمَّ تَرَکَهُ تَعْظِیماً لِلَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ لَمْ یَرْضَ اللَّهُ لَهُ بِمَنْزِلَهٍ یَوْمَ الْقِیَامَهِ إِلَّا مَنْزِلَهَ إِبْرَاهِیمَ خَلِیلِ الرَّحْمَنِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1.  Imam Musa Kadhim (a.s.) narrates that the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) said,
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One who takes a loanee to a ruler and he knows that he has taken a false oath; but due to the sake of the respect of Allah, the Mighty and Sublime, he condones the loanee, Allah would not be satisfied with nothing less than giving  him  the  position of  Ibrahim, the  friend of the Beneficent.”

records.chapter: Reward of being a good teacher
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ahmad b. Muhammad b. Isa and Ibrahim b. Hashim from al-Hussain b. Sayf from his father Sayf b. ’Umayra from ’Amru b. Shimr from Jabir from Abi Ja’far (a.s.) who said: “Every animal walking on this earth, fishes of oceans and every small and big thing in the earth and the heaven repents for a good teacher.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عِیسَی وَ إِبْرَاهِیمَ بْنِ هَاشِمٍ عَنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ سَیْفٍ عَنْ أَبِیهِ سَیْفِ بْنِ عَمِیرَهَ عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ شِمْرٍ عَنْ جَابِرٍ عَنْ أَبِی جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ: مُعَلِّمُ الْخَیْرِ یَسْتَغْفِرُ لَهُ دَوَابُّ الْأَرْضِ وَ حِیتَانُ الْبُحُورِ وَ کُلُّ صَغِیرَهٍ وَ کَبِیرَهٍ فِی أَرْضِ اللَّهِ وَ سَمَائِهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ahmad b. Muhammad b. Isa and Ibrahim b. Hashim from al-Hussain b. Sayf from his father Sayf b. ’Umayra from ’Amru b. Shimr from Jabir from Abi Ja’far (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “Every animal walking on this earth, fishes of oceans and every small and big thing in the earth and the heaven repents for a good teacher.”

records.chapter: Reward of being a good teacher
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 2. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan who said it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan al-Saffar from Muhammad b. Ahmad from Muhammad b. Khalid al-Barqi from the one who mentioned it from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “A single scholar is better than one thousand worshippers and one thousand people who repent. If people benefit from the knowledge of a scholar he is better than seventy thousand worshippers.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ الصَّفَّارُ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ خَالِدٍ الْبَرْقِیِّ عَمَّنْ ذَکَرَهُ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: عَالِمٌ أَفْضَلُ مِنْ أَلْفِ عَابِدٍ وَ أَلْفِ زَاهِدٍ وَ الْعَالِمُ یُنْتَفَعُ بِعِلْمِهِ خَیْرٌ وَ أَفْضَلُ مِنْ عِبَادَهِ سَبْعِینَ أَلْفَ عَابِدٍ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 2. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan who said it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan al-Saffar from Muhammad b. Ahmad from Muhammad b. Khalid al-Barqi from the one who mentioned it from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “A single scholar is better than one thousand worshippers and one thousand people who repent. If people benefit from the knowledge of a scholar he is better than seventy thousand worshippers.”

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) narrates from his ancestors that the  Holy  Prophet  (s.a.w.s.)  said,  “One  who  walks  on  the way to seek knowledge, Allah will make him walk on the way to Paradise. Angels spread their wings for the pleasure of a  student and every  thing  in the  earth and the  heaven including the fishes of oceans repent for him. A scholar is better than a worshipper much in the same way as a full moon is compared to stars. The scholars are inheritors of the prophets  and  the  value  of  inheritance  of  prophets  is measured   in   terms   of   knowledge   instead   of   wealth. Whoever got a little of their inheritance has achieved a lot.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی عَلِیُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِیمَ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مَیْمُونٍ الْقَدَّاحِ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع عَنْ آبَائِهِ ع قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص مَنْ سَلَکَ طَرِیقاً یَطْلُبُ فِیهِ عِلْماً سَلَکَ اللَّهُ بِهِ طَرِیقاً إِلَی الْجَنَّهِ وَ إِنَّ الْمَلَائِکَهَ لَتَضَعُ أَجْنِحَتَهَا لِطَالِبِ الْعِلْمِ رِضًی بِهِ وَ إِنَّهُ یَسْتَغْفِرُ لِطَالِبِ الْعِلْمِ مَنْ فِی السَّمَاوَاتِ وَ مَنْ فِی الْأَرْضِ حَتَّی الْحُوتُ فِی الْبَحْرِ وَ فَضْلُ الْعَالِمِ عَلَی الْعَابِدِ کَفَضْلِ الْقَمَرِ عَلَی سَائِرِ النُّجُومِ لَیْلَهَ الْبَدْرِ وَ إِنَّ الْعُلَمَاءَ وَرَثَهُ الْأَنْبِیَاءِ وَ إِنَّ الْأَنْبِیَاءَ لَمْ یُوَرِّثُوا دِینَاراً وَ لَا دِرْهَماً وَ لَکِنْ وَرَّثُوا الْعِلْمَ فَمَنْ أَخَذَ مِنْهُمْ أَخَذَ بِحَظٍّ وَافِرٍ.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 2. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Ali b. Majluwayh from his uncle Muhammad b. Abi al-Qasim from Muhammad b. Ali al-Kufi from al-Hassan b. Ali b. Yusif from Muqatil from al-Rabee’ b. Muhammad al-Sulami from Jabir b. Yazid al-Ju’fi from Abi Ja’far (a.s.) who said: “No day or night of a scholar ends until he enters the Mercy of Allah. The angels call out, ‘Congratulations, O visitor of Allah! The way, which you are seeking, is the way to Paradise’.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِیِّ بْنِ مَاجِیلَوَیْهِ عَنْ عَمِّهِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِی الْقَاسِمِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِیٍّ الْکُوفِیِّ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِیِّ بْنِ یُوسُفَ عَنْ مُقَاتِلٍ عَنِ الرَّبِیعِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ السُّلَمِیِّ عَنْ جَابِرِ بْنِ یَزِیدَ الْجُعْفِیِّ عَنْ أَبِی جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ: مَا مِنْ عَبْدٍ یَغْدُو فِی طَلَبِ الْعِلْمِ أَوْ یَرُوحُ إِلَّا خَاضَ الرَّحْمَهَ وَ هَتَفَتْ بِهِ الْمَلَائِکَهُ مَرْحَباً بِزَائِرِ اللَّهِ وَ سَلَکَ مِنَ الْجَنَّهِ مِثْلَ ذَلِکَ الْمَسْلَکِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 2. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Ali b. Majluwayh from his uncle Muhammad b. Abi al-Qasim from Muhammad b. Ali al-Kufi from al-Hassan b. Ali b. Yusif from Muqatil from al-Rabee’ b. Muhammad al-Sulami from Jabir b. Yazid al-Ju’fi from Abi Ja’far (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “No day or night of a scholar ends until he enters the Mercy of Allah. The angels call out, ‘Congratulations, O visitor of Allah! The way, which you are seeking, is the way to Paradise’.”

records.chapter: Reward of sitting with religious people
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam  Ja’far  Sadiq  (a.s.)  narrates  that  the  Holy  Prophet (s.a.w.s.) said, “One who sits with religious people will be honored in the world and the hereafter.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُوسَی بْنِ الْمُتَوَکِّلِ عَنْ عَلِیِّ بْنِ الْحُسَیْنِ السَّعْدَآبَادِیِّ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ سَیْفِ بْنِ عَمِیرَهَ عَنْ مَنْصُورِ بْنِ حَازِمٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص مُجَالَسَهُ أَهْلِ الدِّینِ شَرَفُ الدُّنْیَا وَ الْآخِرَهِ.

records.chapter: Reward of performing a deed after knowing its reward
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Ali b. Musa from Ahmad b. Muhammad from Ali b. al-Hakam from Hashim b. Safwan from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “One who performs a deed after he comes to know its reward will get that reward although the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) has said nothing about it.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی عَلِیُّ بْنُ مُوسَی عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ عَلِیِّ بْنِ الْحَکَمِ عَنْ هَاشِمِ بْنِ صَفْوَانَ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ بَلَغَهُ شَیْ ءٌ مِنَ الثَّوَابِ عَلَی شَیْ ءٍ مِنْ خَیْرٍ فَعَمِلَهُ کَانَ لَهُ أَجْرُ ذَلِکَ وَ إِنْ کَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص لَمْ یَقُلْهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Ali b. Musa from Ahmad b. Muhammad from Ali b. al-Hakam from Hashim b. Safwan from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One who performs a deed after he comes to know its reward will get that reward although the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) has said nothing about it.”

records.chapter: Reward of saying a true thing which people follow
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) said, “One who says a true thing and people follow it will also get the reward of people who performed that deed. One who says a false thing and people follow  it, he too will get the punishment for the wrong deed performed by those people.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْبَرْقِیِّ عَمَّنْ رَوَاهُ عَنْ أَبَانٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع لَا یَتَکَلَّمُ الرَّجُلُ بِکَلِمَهِ حَقٍّ فَأُخِذَ بِهَا إِلَّا کَانَ لَهُ مِثْلُ أَجْرِ مَنْ أَخَذَ بِهَا وَ لَا یَتَکَلَّمُ بِکَلِمَهِ ضَلَالٍ یُؤْخَذُ بِهَا إِلَّا کَانَ عَلَیْهِ وِزْرُ مَنْ أَخَذَ بِهَا.

records.chapter: Reward of keeping a good Sunnah alive
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Musa b. al-Mutawakil who said it was narrated to me by Abdillah b. Ja’far al-Himyari from Ahmad b. Muhammad from al-Hassan b. Mahbub from Muawiyah from Maymun al-Qadah from Abi Ja’far (a.s.) who said: “One who enlivens a good Sunnah among people he too gets the reward of people performing that deed and the reward of those people will not diminish. One who starts a wrong practice among the people will get the punishment equal to that of the people following that wrong practice while the punishment of those people won’t be reduced.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُوسَی بْنِ الْمُتَوَکِّلِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ الْحِمْیَرِیُّ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ مُعَاوِیَهَ عَنْ مَیْمُونٍ الْقَدَّاحِ عَنْ أَبِی جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ: أَیُّمَا عَبْدٍ مِنْ عِبَادِ اللَّهِ سَنَّ سُنَّهَ هُدًی کَانَ لَهُ أَجْرٌ مِثْلُ أَجْرِ مَنْ عَمِلَ بِذَلِکَ مِنْ غَیْرِ أَنْ یَنْقُصَ مِنْ أُجُورِهِمْ شَیْ ءٌ وَ أَیُّمَا عَبْدٍ مِنْ عِبَادِ اللَّهِ سَنَّ سُنَّهَ ضَلَالٍ کَانَ عَلَیْهِ مِثْلُ وِزْرِ مَنْ فَعَلَ ذَلِکَ مِنْ غَیْرِ أَنْ یَنْقُصَ مِنْ أَوْزَارِهِمْ شَیْ ءٌ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Musa b. al-Mutawakil who said it was narrated to me by Abdillah b. Ja’far al-Himyari from Ahmad b. Muhammad from al-Hassan b. Mahbub from Muawiyah from Maymun al-Qadah from Abi Ja’far (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One who enlivens a good Sunnah among people he too gets the reward of people performing that deed and the reward of those people will not diminish. One who starts a wrong practice among the people will get the punishment equal to that of the people following that wrong practice while the punishment of those people won’t be reduced.”

records.chapter: Reward of safeguarding orphans, showering mercy on weak, doing good to parents and behaving well with slaves
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ahmad b. Muhammad from al-Hassan b. Ali from Ali b. ’Uqba from Abdillah b. Sinan from Abi Hamzah al-Thumali from Abi Ja’far (a.s.) who said: “Allah builds a house in Paradise for people who do four things – shelter orphans, have mercy on the weak, do good to parents and behave well with slaves.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِیٍّ عَنْ عَلِیِّ بْنِ عُقْبَهَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبِی حَمْزَهَ الثُّمَالِیِّ عَنْ أَبِی جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ: أَرْبَعٌ مَنْ کُنَّ فِیهِ بَنَی اللَّهُ لَهُ بَیْتاً فِی الْجَنَّهِ مَنْ آوَی الْیَتِیمَ وَ رَحِمَ الضَّعِیفَ وَ أَشْفَقَ عَلَی وَالِدَیْهِ وَ رَفَقَ بِمَمْلُوکِهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ahmad b. Muhammad from al-Hassan b. Ali from Ali b. ’Uqba from Abdillah b. Sinan from Abi Hamzah al-Thumali from Abi Ja’far (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “Allah builds a house in Paradise for people who do four things – shelter orphans, have mercy on the weak, do good to parents and behave well with slaves.”
